title: Continuous Ingestion, Reindexing, Cutover, and Disaster Recovery · Program Brief: Rag Ingestion Retry Deadletter
summary: A nightly job rebuilds the live index in place, loses failed documents, publishes partial results, misses deletes, and has backups that were never restored. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ingestion retry deadletter increment in recoverable continuous-indexing system. Learners progress through prediction…
objectives: Classify parse, policy, model, rate, storage, poison, and unknown failures with bounded retries, dead letters, replay identity, and operator evidence.
Capture creates, updates, moves, permissions, supersession, deletes, checkpoints, source snapshots, and out-of-order events from connectors.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-ingestion-retry-deadletter — Classify parse, policy, model, rate, storage, poison, and unknown failures with bounded retries, dead letters, replay identity, and operator evidence.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-ingestion-operations-recovery-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-ingestion-operations-recovery-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_ingestion_operations_recovery_4(source_version: int = 14, indexed_version: int = 14, failed_documents: int = 0, dead_letters: int = 0, blue_green_ready: bool = True, restore_verified: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if source_version < 1 or indexed_version < 1:
        return False, 0, "version-input"
    if indexed_version != source_version:
        return False, indexed_version, "index-lag"
    if failed_documents != dead_letters:
        return False, failed_documents, "failure-accounting"
    if not blue_green_ready:
        return False, indexed_version, "unsafe-cutover"
    if not restore_verified:
        return False, indexed_version, "unproven-recovery"
    assert failed_documents == dead_letters
    return True, indexed_version, "recoverable-index"

# Evidence: rag-ingestion-retry-deadletter-1yx8e4
# Competency: rag-ingestion-retry-deadletter.
# Case 8086: a public-benefits policy change.
# Operating constraint: keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence.
# Changed case: change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence.
def evidence_rag_ingestion_retry_deadletter_1yx8e4(source_version: int = 14, indexed_version: int = 14, failed_documents: int = 0, dead_letters: int = 0, blue_green_ready: bool = True, restore_verified: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1yx8e4 = "1yx8e4-4e8xy1-8086"
    assert evidence_variant_1yx8e4
    evidence_axes_1yx8e4 = (
        "corpus1yx8e4",
        "query4e8xy1",
        "tenant8086",
        "index1yx8e48086",
        "prompt4e8xy18086",
        "evaluation80861yx8e4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1yx8e4)) == 6
    if source_version < 1 or indexed_version < 1:
        return False, 0, "version-input"
    if indexed_version != source_version:
        return False, indexed_version, "index-lag"
    if failed_documents != dead_letters:
        return False, failed_documents, "failure-accounting"
    if not blue_green_ready:
        return False, indexed_version, "unsafe-cutover"
    if not restore_verified:
        return False, indexed_version, "unproven-recovery"
    assert failed_documents == dead_letters
    return True, indexed_version, "recoverable-index"
